def: s.f. corr. of tabāhī, q.v. s.f. Ruin, wreck, destruction, perdition; wretchedness, misery; wickedness, depravity
subs: v.n. To experience misery; to be afflicted.

def: s.f. Tribes, peoples, nations; troops, bands (particularly of dancing girls); — s.f. sing. A dancing girl.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَوَائِف (tawā'if), ultimately borrowed from Arabic طَوائِف (ṭawāʔif).

ety: inf. n. v of وجه 'to turn the face (to)'
def: s.f. Directing the steps (towards), turning (towards or to); countenancing, regarding, attending (to); inclination; regard; attention, consideration, countenance, favour, kindness, obligingness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوَجُّه (tawajju), from Arabic تَوَجُّه (tawajjuh). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi توجہ (tvjh /⁠tavajjuh⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of tawajjǒh), Attentions, favours, kindnesses, &c.

ety: inf. n. vi of وجد 'to be transported'
def: s.m. Mutual ecstasy, rapture.

ety: inf. n. v of وقع 'to fall'
def: s.f. Expectation, hope; trust, reliance; wish, desire; request
subs: (-kī, of a thing, -se, from a person), To entertain or have hope, to hope; to expect, to look (for), to desire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian توقع (tawaqqu'), from Arabic تَوَقُّع (tawaqquʕ).

ety: inf. n. v of وقف 'to stand, stop,' &c.
def: s.m. Stopping, pausing; pause, cessation; delay, suspension; hesitation; tediousness; patience
subs: v.n. To pause, hesitate, wait, delay, dally, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of وکل 'to entrust' (to), &c.
def: s.m. Trusting (to), depending (upon); trust in God; faith, reliance; resignation (to the Divine will)
subs: To rest in hope, to trust in God, to be resigned; to sit idle, to do nothing.

ety: inf. n. v of وقر 'to be mild,' &c.
def: s.m. Mildness, modesty, gentleness; sedateness, gravity; respect, dignity, honour.
adv. Rapidly, fast, quick.
s.m. = taṅwālā, q.v. s.m. Darkness before the eyes, fainting, a swoon.

ety: prob. for A. t̤uwālat, fem. of t̤uwāl = t̤awīl; — or for t̤awīlat
def: s.f. Prolonging; length, protractedness; prolixity.

ety: inf. n. vi of ولي 'to be near or contiguous'
def: s.f. Following in succession; continuation, succession.
s.m. = tāʼolā; utāwalā, q.v. adv. Rapidly, fast, quick.

ety: for A. tawallī, inf. n. v of ولي 'to be near,' &c.
def: s.m. Affection, attachment; cultivating friendship; hope.

ety: inf. n. v of ولد 'to bear children'
def: s.m. Birth, nativity; — adj. Born, generated
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوَلُّد (tawallud), from Arabic تَوَلُّد (tawallud).

ety: inf. n. vi of ولد 'to bear children'
def: s.m. Begetting, being born generation after generation; propagation, generating again and again.
s.m. A twin ( = joṛlā).
s.m. dual (of tauʼam), Twins (syn. joṛle).
s.m. Satisfaction, compensation, amends, recompense, retaliation; penalty, forfeit, fine, mulct; fine for bloodshed (opp. to qiṣāṣ, q.v.); — debt; a loan
subs: tāwān lagānā, tāwān lenā, v.n. To fine, mulct, amerce
(-ko), To cause compensation to be made, to adjudge damages
(-ko), To make amends, pay a penalty.

def: adj. See s.v. tawān. s.m. Satisfaction, compensation, amends, recompense, retaliation; penalty, forfeit, fine, mulct; fine for bloodshed (opp. to qiṣāṣ, q.v.); — debt; a loan
subs: tāwān lagānā, tāwān lenā, v.n. To fine, mulct, amerce
(-ko), To cause compensation to be made, to adjudge damages
(-ko), To make amends, pay a penalty.

def: adj. = tawāngar, q.v. adj. See s.v. tawān. s.m. Satisfaction, compensation, amends, recompense, retaliation; penalty, forfeit, fine, mulct; fine for bloodshed (opp. to qiṣāṣ, q.v.); — debt; a loan
subs: tāwān lagānā, tāwān lenā, v.n. To fine, mulct, amerce
(-ko), To cause compensation to be made, to adjudge damages
(-ko), To make amends, pay a penalty.

def: s.f. = tawāngarī, q.v.s.v. tawān.
s.f. Compensation, &c. ( = tāwān).
s.m. = taṅwar, and tomar, qq.v. s.m. Copper ( = tāṅbā, q.v.). s.m. Copper
subs: s.m. A copper rupee, a bad rupee
s.m. Sulphate of copper, blue vitriol.

def: s.m. See s.v. ta. s.m. A tail, the breast; the womb; the hip or flank; a warrior; a thief; a wicked man; an outcast, a barbarian; a jewel; nectar; a tree; — passing, crossing.
s.m. See s.v. ṭa. s.m. A tail, the breast; the womb; the hip or flank; a warrior; a thief; a wicked man; an outcast, a barbarian; a jewel; nectar; a tree; — passing, crossing.
s.f. pl. (of tārīḵẖ), Dates; chronicles, histories, annals.

ety: inf. n. vi of ورد 'to enter'
def: s.m. Unintentionally inserting the sense of another's verses in one's own poems; — corresponding (by letter).

ety: inf. n. v of وسل not in use
def: s.m. Introduction (to another person; conjunction; copulation (cf wasīla).

ety: inf. n. v of وسط 'to sit in the midst'
def: s.m. Mediation; introduction; middle-man, mediator, umpire.
adj. & adv. So great; so much, so many; such; so far, so long; — so greatly, to such an extent; until.

ety: inf. n. v of وطن 'to dwell, abide'
def: s.m. Fixing one's residence (in a place or country), taking up one's abode (in); residence.

ety: inf. n. vi of وتر 'to separate,' &c.
def: s.m. Following in succession; succession, continuation.

ety: inf. n. vi of وضع 'to place, lay down'
def: s.f. Humility; attention, civility, courtesy; hospitality, reception, entertainment; gift, present; pretended kindness, empty compliments
subs: (-kī), To treat with civility or courtesy, to receive with politeness; to entertain; to offer (a thing) as a present, to make a present (to).

ety: inf. n. v. of وضأ 'to excel in cleanliness,' &c.'
def: s.f. Performing ablutions before prayer; arriving at the age of puberty (boys or girls) when ablution must be performed.

ety: inf. n. ii of عوق 'to hinder; to cleave (to)'
def: s.f. Hindering, preventing; averting; delaying; suspending, suspension.

ety: inf. n. ii of اول 'to revert,' &c.
def: s.f. Explanation, exposition, elucidation, interpretation; paraphrase; turning (language) from the obvious meaning, interpreting in a manner not according to the obvious meaning
subs: (-kī), To explain, &c.; to interpret (verses, dreams, &c.); to turn (language) from the obvious meaning.

ety: v.n. fr. طول 'to be or become long'; see t̤ūl
def: adj. Long; tall; prolix; diffuse.

ety: for A. طويلة, fem. of t̤awīl, q.v.
def: s.m. A long rope with which cattle are tied; a tether, foot-band, fetter; — a stable, stall.

ety: inf. n. ii of عوذ 'to seek protection'
def: s.m. Praying for protection or preservation (by invoking God, or by uttering some charm), having recourse to God; — a charm, an amulet, a phylactery; a magic square; a structure of brick or stone work over a grave; a tombstone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْوِیذ (ta'wīz), borrowed from Arabic تَعْوِيذ (taʕwīḏ).

def: s.m. Paternal uncle ( = tāʼū, q.v.). s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi تایا (taya /⁠tāyā⁠/) (c. 1435), from Sanskrit *तातिय (*tātiya) / तात्य (tātyá). Cognate with Punjabi تایْا (tāyā) / ਤਾਇਆ (tāiā).

def: s.m. = tawā; and taʼī, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. v of يقن 'to know for certain'
def: s.m. Knowing for certain, ascertaining; ascertainment; certainty (cf. yaqīn).

ety: inf. n. v of يقظ 'to wake, be watchful'
def: s.m. Waking, watchfulness, vigilance; lucubration.

ety: inf. n. v of ام 'to betake oneself (to)'
def: s.m. lit. 'Betaking oneself to dust, to wipe the face and hands and arms therewith, for prayer'; purifying or rubbing the hands, face, and other parts of the body before prayer, with sand or dust, where water cannot be had.
wety: From Arabic تَيَمُّم (tayammum).

ety: S. तार्क्ष्य+रः; or तर्तरीकः, rt. तॄ
def: s.m. = H ٿايري टायरी tāyarī, ṭāʼerī, s.f. A horse; a hack.
s.m. Flying, flight (of birds, &c.).

ety: S. तार्क्ष्य+रः; or तर्तरीकः, rt. तॄ
def: s.m. A horse; a hack.
s.f. The wife of a father-in-law's elder brother.
s.m. Father-in-law's elder brother.
adj. Running, hastening, &c. (used in comp.); — s.f. Running, race; — assault, attack, invasion (cf. tāzī)
subs: s.f. Labour and exertion, toil and trouble (syn. dauṛ-dhūp).

ety: fr. taz; Zend taćañtan, rt. tać = S. तञ्च्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Fresh, new, recent; young, tender; raw, green; fat, plump, in good condition; well-off, prosperous; blooming; pleased, happy
subs: adj. Fresh and plump; young and strong
adj. & s.m. Imagining something fresh or new; — an inventor
s.m. Fresh strength; good spirits, hilarity, cheerfulness; — tāza-dam honā, v.n. To gain fresh strength, be refreshed; to be in good spirits, &c.
adj. & s.m. Making fresh, making anew; — beginner; renewer
s.f. Fresh or new work; new building
v.t. To make fresh, freshen, refresh, recreate; to renew, renovate, restore
s.m. New comer, stranger, foreigner; — simpleton, green-horn
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَازَه (tāza).

ety: inf. n. ii of ذبذب 'to put in motion,' &c.
def: s.m. Commotion, agitation, palpitation; wavering, vacillation; suspension of judgment; perplexity; ambiguity.

ety: inf. n. vi of ضدّ 'to overcome'
def: s.m. Contrariety, opposition, contradiction; inconsistency, absurdity.
s.f. Freshness, newness, novelty; renewal, renovation, revival; fatness, plumpness, good condition; tenderness; greenness; pleasure.

ety: inf. n. v of ظلم 'to act tyrannically'
def: s.m. Complaining of the wrong-doing, injustice, or tyranny (of anyone); groaning under oppression; injus tice, injury, oppression.

ety: inf. n. ii of زلزل 'to shake,' &c.
def: s.m. Being shaken, being or becoming in a state of motion; commotion, agitation, trepidation; an earthquake; — swerving (from the path of rectitude); discrepancy (in a deposition, &c.); defalcation.

ety: inf. n. v. of ضرع 'to be humbled,' &c.
def: s.m. Self-abasement, humility; earnest supplication; complaining, lamenting.
s.m. = tadarv, q.v. s.m. A cock pheasant, a pheasant (to whose gait that of a mistress is compared).
s.m. = A تزويج tazwīj [inf. n. ii of زوج 'to pair'], s.f. Marrying, taking a partner or wife.

ety: inf. n. vi of زيد 'to increase'
def: s.m. Increasing; increase, augmentation.
s.m. A tāzī or Arab colt.
s.f. The art of gilding; illumination (of manuscripts).

ety: inf. n. ii of ضحك 'to laugh'
def: s.f. Laughing at, mocking; ridicule, derision.
s.f. contract. of the next, q.v.

r: tāzī, tāz, lit, tāzī zabān, tāzī-ḵẖāna
def: s.m. Dog-kennel
s.f. Punishment; torture.

ety: inf. n. ii of ضعف 'to be weak'
def: s.f. Folding over; doubling.
s.m. A man or a horse of Arab blood born and brought up in another country; a horse of mixed breed.

ety: inf. n. ii of عزل 'to set aside, to remove'
def: s.f. Removing or deposing (from office), displacing.
s.f. Magnifying, honouring, treating with respect, reverence, or veneration; honour, respect, reverence; geniality, politeness
subs: (-kī), To show respect (to), to honour, respect, or reverence.

def: s.f. Making an excuse, apologizing; excuse, subterfuge. (Rare.)

ety: inf. n. ii of عزر 'to beat, to punish'
def: s.f. Punishment, correction, reproof, censure, reprimand.
s.m. A representation of the shrines of Hasan and Husain, sons of 'Alī (which is carried in procession at the Moḥarram by the Shīʻas chiefly)
subs: To bury a taʻziya, to throw a taʻziya into the river (at the anniversary of the Moḥarram)
s.m. The place of the taʻziya
taʻziya-gīr, s.m. One who observes the mourning in Moḥarram
s.f. Making a taʻziya; the observance of Moḥarram
v.n. To observe the mourning during Moḥarram, to go into mourning.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَعْزِيَة (taʕziya).

def: s.m. A whip, scourge; flogging, corporal punishment
subs: tāziyāna lagānā, tāziyāna mārnā, v.t. To lay the whip on, to flog, to scourge.

ety: for A. تعزية, inf. n. ii of عزي 'to endure patiently'
def: s.f. Consoling; condolence; lamentation, mourning
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْزِیَت (ta'ziyat), from Arabic تَعْزِيَة (taʕziya).

def: s.f. Bringing to memory; mentioning, recording; commemoration; exhortation, admonition; — making masculine; the masculine gender.

ety: for A. تذکرة, inf. n. ii of ذکر 'to remember'
def: s.f. Memoirs of eminent men.

ety: for A. تذکرة, inf. n. ii of ذکر 'to remember'
def: s.f. Memoirs of eminent men.
adv. By way of reminding; by way of mention; by way of memoir.
s.m. Purifying; causing to grow, thrive, or increase; purification.

ety: inf. n. ii of ذل 'to be base,' &c.
def: s.f. Debasing, degrading, humiliating; bringing under subjection; debasement, abasement, humiliation; depression.

ety: inf. n. ii of ضمن 'to be responsible,' &c.
def: s.f. Taking (one) as a surety or guarantee; giving security; giving satisfaction (for an injury); entrusting (a thing, &c.) with another; lending on interest; causing (a thing) to be comprehended or contained (in another); comprehending or including (one thing in another); putting or depositing (a thing) in (a receptacle); inserting or introducing a verse or two (of another poet) into one's own poem (to complete, or to corroborate, the meaning intended, — notifying it, however, as borrowed)
subs: v.t. To insert a verse or two (of another poet) in one's own poem, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of زوج 'to pair'
def: s.f. = A تزوج tazawwuj [inf. n. v of زوج], s.m. Marrying, taking a partner or wife.

ety: inf. n. ii of زور; but prob. from zūr, 'falsehood'
def: s.f. Falsifying; falsehood, lie; deception, deceit, fraud, imposture, stratagem.

ety: inf. n. ii of ضيع 'to perish'
def: s.f. Causing to perish, destroying, spoiling, wasting, idling away (time)
subs: s.f. Idling away time; ennui, weariness.

ety: inf. n. ii of زين 'to adorn'
def: s.f. Adorning, decorating, dressing (oneself); decoration, ornament, jewel; honour.

postpn. (old H.) = se, q.v. The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. Its sound in Arabic is that of th in the English thin; but in Hindūstānī it has the sound of s. According to the abjad, q.v., it has the numerical value of 500.

r: te, ta, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, Hindī, t, abjad, ta-kār
def: s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit -इत (-ita).

r: te, tā, wǒh, te-tā, jetā, tak
def: adj. & adv. So much, that much; so many, that many
subs: te-hī, pron. To him, &c. ( = tis-ko); — adv. There and then, immediately, instantly.

ety: for ṭewā, perf. part. of ṭewnā
def: part. adj. Sharpened.
s.m. A long stitch (cf. ṭappā; tepći).

ety: Zend, tigha, rt. tij = S. तिज्
def: s.f. A sword, scimitar; cutlass; dagger
subs: adj. & s.m. Striking with a sword; using a sword; — one who uses a sword or scimitar, a swordsman
s.f. Use of the sword or scimitar
v.t. To put to the sword.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تیغ (tēġ), possibly either derived from Parthian [script needed] (tyg /⁠tēγ⁠/, “blade; sword, lance”); or from Middle Persian [script needed] (tyh /⁠tēx⁠/, “sharp edge, ridge; ray”).

def: s.m. A short, broad scimitar; a broadsword; — name of a trick in wrestling; — a walled-up arch or other opening
subs: s.m. A sword-player; a swordsman; a hero
s.m. A sword like a scimitar.

def: s.m. A short, broad scimitar; a broadsword; — name of a trick in wrestling; — a walled-up arch or other opening
subs: s.m. A sword-player; a swordsman; a hero
s.m. A sword like a scimitar.

def: v.n. (prov.) = ṭighalnā, q.v. v.n. To be fused or melted, to melt, dissolve; to rarefy.
s.m. = ṭekrā, q.v. s.m. Heap, mound, hillock, rising ground; slope or declivity (of a hill).

ety: Prk. तेओ (with euphonic h); S. तेजस्
def: s.m. Anger, wrath, passion, rage, vehemence of anger; speaking with warmth, vehemence of manner, peremptoriness, imperiousness; perseverance, steadfastness.

ety: Prk. तेओ (with euphonic h); S. तेजस्
def: s.m. Anger, wrath, passion, rage, vehemence of anger; speaking with warmth, vehemence of manner, peremptoriness, imperiousness; perseverance, steadfastness.
s.f. A kind of ornament worn by women round the ankles.
s.m. = ṭehrā, and = tehrā, qq.v. adj. Three-fold, treble, triple, triplicate (see tehrā).
adj. = tehrā, q.v.; cf. also tihāṛā. adj. Three-fold, treble, triple, triplicate (see tehrā).
pron. = terā (an old and poetio form).
adv. Exactly then, &c. (see s.v. te). s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

def: s.m. Marriage custom, marriage caremony.
adj. Three-fold, treble, triple, triplicate (see tehrā).
adj. (f. -ī), Threefold, triplicate, &c. = تہرا těhrā, q.v.
s.m. A small village, a hamlet ( = purwā).
s.m. A village.

ety: těhrā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To tertiate; to do (a thing) for the third time; to triple, to make three-fold.
s.f. Triplication.
s.m. = teʼohār, q.v. s.m. A holiday, a festival (cf. těhwār and itwār).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi تیوہار (tyvhar /⁠tiyohār⁠/) (c. 1503), from Apabhramsa तिहिवार (tihivāra), from Sanskrit *तिथि-वार (*tithi-vāra).

def: adj. Twenty-three.
s.m. Sharpness, pungency; spirit; energy; strength, power; ardour, fire; wrath; splendour, glory, refulgence, awe; — tej-pāt (S. teja+patra), s.m. The leaf of the Laurus cassia, cassia, bay-leaf
subs: (f. -matī), tej-vān, tej-vant (f. -vatī), adj. & s.m. Sharp, pungent; energetic; strong; glorious, refulgent, splendid, luminous, brilliant, awful, majestic; famous, celebrated; — tej-vatī, s.f. The aromatic plant Pothos officinalis.
wety: From Sanskrit तृतीया (tṛtīyā).

def: s.f. The plant Aletris hyacinthoides.
s.m. Flame, fire; glow, glare; light, lustre, radiance, brilliance, splendour, glory; beauty; dignity, consequence; strength, power (cf. tej); violence; semen virile
subs: adj. Irradiating, illuminating; conferring strength, power, or glory; restorative, invigorating, provocative, tonic; — s.m. The sun
adj. & s.m. Whose form is light; consisting wholly of splendour; — the nature of light; the supreme spirit, Brahma
s.m. The tree Premna spinosa (the friction of the wood engendering flame)
adj. Consisting of splendour or light, shining, brilliant, luminous, glorious; full of energy or ardour
s.f. The plant Piper chaba; the plant Scindapsus officinalis.

def: adj. = tej-mān, q.v.s.v. tej.
adj. = tej-mān, q.v.s.v. tej.
v.t. (prov.) = tyajnā, or tajnā, q.v. v.t. To abandon, give up, relinquish, quit, leave, forsake, desert
s.m. A kind of reed, Saccharum sara.

see s.v. tejas for compounds of which tejo is first member. s.m. Flame, fire; glow, glare; light, lustre, radiance, brilliance, splendour, glory; beauty; dignity, consequence; strength, power (cf. tej); violence; semen virile
subs: adj. Irradiating, illuminating; conferring strength, power, or glory; restorative, invigorating, provocative, tonic; — s.m. The sun
adj. & s.m. Whose form is light; consisting wholly of splendour; — the nature of light; the supreme spirit, Brahma
s.m. The tree Premna spinosa (the friction of the wood engendering flame)
adj. Consisting of splendour or light, shining, brilliant, luminous, glorious; full of energy or ardour
s.f. The plant Piper chaba; the plant Scindapsus officinalis.

def: adj. See s.v. tej. s.m. Sharpness, pungency; spirit; energy; strength, power; ardour, fire; wrath; splendour, glory, refulgence, awe; — tej-pāt (S. teja+patra), s.m. The leaf of the Laurus cassia, cassia, bay-leaf
subs: (f. -matī), tej-vān, tej-vant (f. -vatī), adj. & s.m. Sharp, pungent; energetic; strong; glorious, refulgent, splendid, luminous, brilliant, awful, majestic; famous, celebrated; — tej-vatī, s.f. The aromatic plant Pothos officinalis.

def: s.m. Mass, pile, heap, mound, hillock ( = ṭekar); obstacle, bar, hindrance.
s.f. Prop, pillar, support; reliance; promise, vow; determination, resolve; the burthen or refrain (of a song)
subs: v.n. To be bent (on), be determined or resolved (on).

def: s.m. Pillar, prop, support.

ety: ṭek+Prk. अडो or अडओ; S. रः or र+कः
def: s.m. Heap, mound, hillock, rising ground; slope or declivity (of a hill).
adj. Supported, reliable, trustworthy, certain, positive (syn. niśćay-yut); — s.f. = thāpī, q.v.

ety: ṭek˚ = Prk. टायक्क(इ) = S. त्राय (rt. त्रै)+कृ
def: v.t. To support, prop; to place, set or put down
s.f. dim. of ṭekan, qq.v.

ety: ṭek+Prk. अडो or अडओ; S. रः or र+कः
def: s.m. Heap, mound, hillock, rising ground; slope or declivity (of a hill).
adj. Oily, unctuous; dark, deep (colour); — s.m. A dark bay colour
subs: adj. & s.m. Light bay coloured (a horse); — a light bay

def: v.t. To push, thrust in ( = ṭhelnā; ghuseṛnā); to remove ( = ṭālnā).
s.f. A small oil-pot.
s.m. A beam.
s.f. (m. in S.), Wetness, moisture; freshness, verdure.
s.f. Snuff or flame (of a candle or lamp).
s.m. Wetting, moistening; wet, damp, moisture; a sauce, condiment.
s.f. A sort of chimney; a fire-place.
pron. A dialectic form of tū, 'thou.'
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi तीनि (tīni), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀺𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀺 (tiṇṇi), from Sanskrit त्रीणि n (trīṇi).

def: postp. (prov.) = taʼīṅ, q.v. s.f. = tāʼī; — postpn. = taʼīṅ; qq.v. s.f. The wife of a father's elder brother; paternal uncle's wife; a term of respectful compellation or mention of a female generally.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi तीनि (tīni), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀺𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀺 (tiṇṇi), from Sanskrit त्रीणि n (trīṇi).

def: postp. (prov.) = te, or se, q.v. s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi तीनि (tīni), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀺𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀺 (tiṇṇi), from Sanskrit त्रीणि n (trīṇi).

def: s.m. A cock of the breed of fowls called ṭenī.
v.t. = ṭeʼonā, q.v.
s.m. The resinous fruit of the tree Diospyros embryopteris, or D. glutinosa.
s.m. (f. -ī), A large species of leopard or panther, Felis leopardus.
s.f. Teasing.
s.m. A kind of fish (Silurus).
s.m. A kind of fish (Silurus).
adj. & s.m. Tiny, little, small; — a breed of small fowls; a bantam.
s.f.m. (prov.) = ṭek, q.v. s.f. Prop, pillar, support; reliance; promise, vow; determination, resolve; the burthen or refrain (of a song)
subs: v.n. To be bent (on), be determined or resolved (on).
wety: Borrowed from English tank.

def: s.m. The ripe fruit of karīl, q.v.; — film, outer crust or covering; speck in the eye, cataract ( = phullī); — a cotton-pod.
s.m. A large ripe fruit of karīl; a fizgig, a kind of fire-work (syn. phuljhaṛī); inconsiderate speech.
adj. = tetālīs, q.v. adj. Forty-three.
s.m. (prov.) The fruit of the karīl ( = ṭeṅṭ).
s.f. An imitative, or an inarticulate sound; the screech or cry of a parrot (or other bird, syn. ćeṅ-ćeṅ); murmuring, muttering, babbling, prating
subs: v.n. To utter an inarticulate sound; to screech (as a parrot); to prate, babble; to murmur, mutter.

def: s.f. The fruit of the karīl; — inconsiderate speech.
adj. = tetīs, q.v. adj. Thirty-three.
s.m. The windpipe, the throat; the head (of a snake)
subs: (-kā), To compress the windpipe, to throttle, strangle.

def: s.m. The knee; the ankle.
s.m. A holiday, a festival (cf. těhwār and itwār).
adj. & s.f. Relating or pertaining to a festival; — a present made at a festival.
s.f. A pillar, prop, support ( = ṭek; ṭekan).
s.m. The third year past or to come; the year before last; the year after next.
s.f. = teʼorī, q.v. s.f. A contracted brow, a frown, scowl
subs: v.n. = teʼorī ćaṛhānā, v.n. To raise or to knit the brow, to frown
v.n. The brow to be knit, a frown to come on
v.n. To contract or knit the brow, &c. = teʼorī ćaṛhānā.

def: s.f. Basting (a garment, &c.); a running stitch; a felling, a seam hemmed down
subs: (-meṅ), To put in running stitches, to run, to baste; to fell a seam.

r: te-pi, te, api, we bhī, tau bhī, tau
def: (te, q.v.+S. api), Braj for we bhī; also = tau bhī, q.v.s.v. tau. s.m. Father's elder brother, paternal uncle; — an elder, superior; — an astute fellow, a knave.
adj. Passed, spent (as time)
subs: v.n. To be passed or spent.
wety: From Sanskrit तीर (tīra).

def: part. adj. Passed, spent ( = ter, q.v.)
s.f. Voice, sound; cry, call, shout; tune.

ety: S. ते, gen. of त्वं, Prk. ते or तइ+rā, gen. aff.
def: pron. (gen. sing. of tū), Thy, thine
subs: s.m. terī-merī, s.f. Angry thy-ing and -my-ing, altercation; — terā-merā karnā, v.n. To squabble or dispute (about a thing), to quarrel (over a thing).
wety: From Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀏 (tae) + را (rā, genitive affix), the first element of which is from Sanskrit ते (te), variant dative of त्वम् (tvam). Cognate with Classical Persian ترا (turā): تو (tū) + را (rā)

def: s.m. The trunk of a tree; an instrument for twisting coarse thread or twine (cf. aṭeran).
s.m. A curtain; — adj. Squint-eyed.
adj. Thirteen
subs: s.m. The first thirteen days of the Arabian month Safar (held unlucky because Mohammad was seriously ill during those days).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑁂𑀭𑀲 (terasa).

def: adj. = next, q.v.
adj. (f. -īṅ), Thirteenth; — terhawīṅ, s.f. The thirteenth day after a death (when the last of the funeral rites is performed).
(see next), s.m.(?) Calling, shouting, hallooing.
s.f. The thirteenth day of the lunar fortnight ( = tirodaśī).
adj. = terah-wāṅ, q.v. adj. (f. -īṅ), Thirteenth; — terhawīṅ, s.f. The thirteenth day after a death (when the last of the funeral rites is performed).
adj. (prov.) = ṭeṛhā; — s.f. Twist; pride, &c. ( = ṭeṛhī).
adj. (f. -ī), Crooked, bent, distorted, awry, askew; deformed, hump-backed, bow-legged, &c.; offended, displeased, cranky; froward, obstinate; untoward, unfavourable, contrary, opposing, obstructive
subs: v.t. To misunderstand, misapprehend ( = ulṭā samajhnā)
(-ko), To give crooked answers, to give cross or rough answers, to be impertinent or rude (to)
v.t. To make crooked, to bend, crook, distort
v.n. To be crooked; to be froward, or perverse, &c.; to be displeased or offended (with, -se).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi टेढौ (ṭeḍhau), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀢𑁆𑀭𑁂𑀟𑁆𑀟 (*treḍḍa).

ety: ṭeṛhā, q.v.+pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) or त्व+कं; and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Crookedness, distortion, obliquity, tortuousness.

ety: ṭeṛhā, q.v.+pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) or त्व+कं; and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Crookedness, distortion, obliquity, tortuousness.
s.f. Frowardness, perverseness, obstinacy; pride, vanity, haughtiness ( = akaṛ).

ety: ṭeṛhā, q.v.+pan, or panā = Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं) or त्व+कं; and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Crookedness, distortion, obliquity, tortuousness.

s.f. A list or string of classified amounts to be added together; the paper containing the same; an item in a number of items set down to be added together; summing up and finding the grand total of the several amounts under one head; the grand total thus obtained; a classified list or account; a register.
v.n. To call aloud, cry out, to shout, scream, bawl, roar; to to sing, chant; — v.t. To twist the keys (of an instrument), to tune.
s.m. = teʼoras, q.v. s.m. The third year past or to come; the year before last; the year after next.

ety: Zend tasha, rt. tash; S. तक्ष
def: s.m. A carpenter's axe; an adze
subs: (-ko), To strike with an adze; to use an adze.

def: s.f. Thrice-ploughed land.
s.m. The tree Butea frondosa (syn. palās); the blossom of the Butea; an effigy of the head of Tesu (a mythical hero) carried about in the month Ásin; a kind of play.
s.m. A tear (see ṭaswe).
adj. & adv. (correl. to je-tā); see s.v. te. s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

def: adj. & s.m. Consisting of three parts or divisions; three-storied; a three-storied house.
adj. Forty-three.
pron. (Braj, or old H.) = te, q.v.; — adj. & adv. = ati; bahut; qq.v. s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

def: adj. & adv. (correl. to jetik) = tetā, q.v.s.v. te. adj. & adv. (correl. to je-tā); see s.v. te. s.m. The letter or sound t
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

def: adj. Thirty-three.
s.m. = ṭeṅṭuʼā or ṭeṅṭwā, q.v. s.m. The windpipe, the throat; the head (of a snake)
subs: (-kā), To compress the windpipe, to throttle, strangle.

def: pron. (Braj) emphatic form of te, q.v. (common in poetry).
s.f. Habit, custom, way, manner; trick; whim, fancy.
s.m. Habit, &c. = ṭeʼo, q.v.; calculation of nativity; a small horoscope.
s.m. Play, sport, pastime; a garden, pleasure-garden, play-ground.
s.m. Darkness before the eyes, swimming in the head, giddiness, vertigo fainting; — peculiar appearance or expression of the eyes; eye; lock, aspect, expression, countenance, physiogamy; the brow
subs: tewar badalnā. v.n. To change countenance
to change colour; to change (one's) conduct (towards), to turn away (one's) regard or affection
v.n. To undergo a change of expression or countenance, to change (one's) expression in death.

def: v.t. To sharpen, to whet.

ety: tewar˚, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To have a swimming in the head, to be giddy, to be faint; to stagger
subs: v.n. To fall down senseless or in a swoon.

ety: Old P. tezh; Pehl. tej; Zend, tizhin, rt. tij = S. tij
def: adj. Sharp, keen, acute; penetrating, piercing (glance, &c.); hot, pungent, strong, acrid; caustic, corrosive; fiery, passionate, impetuous, violent; swift, fleet; quick, apt, intelligent, keen-witted; high-priced, dear
subs: tez-rau, adj. Fast-going, swift-paced, rapid, fleet
tez-rauʼī, s.f. Swiftness, fleetness, speed; — tez-rauʼī karnā, v.n. To practise swiftness; to attempt speed
adj. Quick of apprehension, of an acute intellect or genius
tez-fahm, adj. Of quick understanding, quick of apprehension, intelligent, acute, apt
s.f. Quickness of apprehension, intelligence, acumen, acuteness, aptness
tez-gām, adj. Swift-paced, fast-going, fleet; active, brisk, quick
tez-nigāh, adj. Sharp-sighted, of keen or penetrating vision, keen-eyed
adj. Sharp-witted, sharp, intelligent, &c. = tez-fahm, q.v.
v.n. To be sharpened, to be whetted (on, -par); to fly into a passion, become violent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تیز (tēz).

def: s.m. Cress, cresses, Lepidium sativum.

s.f. Sharpness, keenness; pungency, acrimony; heat, warmth; fieriness, fury, anger, wrath; violence, impetuosity; force, intensity; spirit, fire, fervour, ardour; quickness, acuteness, wit, intelligence; incisiveness, edge, point; velocity, swiftness, fleetness, speed; scarcity, dearth; dearness, high price.
Was, (irreg. past tense masc. sing. of the verb honā, 'to be'; — fem. sing. thī: see Gram.).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi था (thā), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀣𑀺𑀬 (thiya), from Sanskrit स्थित (sthitá), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *stʰHtás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *stʰHtás, from Proto-Indo-European *sth₂tós. Cognate with Gujarati થયો (thayo) and Latin stātus (whence French été (“been”)).

def: s.f. = thāh, q.v. s.f. Bottom; depth; end; ford
subs: (-kī), To take the depth (of), to sound.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi था (thā), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀣𑀺𑀬 (thiya), from Sanskrit स्थित (sthitá), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *stʰHtás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *stʰHtás, from Proto-Indo-European *sth₂tós. Cognate with Gujarati થયો (thayo) and Latin stātus (whence French été (“been”)).

def: s.m. A loud sound, sound made by one thing striking against another.
s.m. Place, position, station ( = sthān, and used chiefly in comp.); (in Mus.) andante.
s.m. The letter or the sound thā.
s.m. The letter or sound ṭh.
s.m. The back stick of a paper kite; the back-bone
subs: adj. & s.m. 'Having the back-bone bent or broken,' hump-backed; — a hunch-back.

def: s.m. Wrangling, altercation, squabble, quarrel, broil ( = bakheṛā; jhagrā).
s.m. A robber, assassin, garotter, cut-throat; one of a gang who strangle or poison travellers; deceiver, impostor, cheat, knave, sharper, swindler, plunderer
subs: s.f. Cunning, trickery, roguery, fraudulent dealing, &c. (see ṭhagāʼī)
s.f. The art of tricking or cheating; fraudulent arts, swindling, &c. (see ṭhagāʼī).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀞𑀕 (ṭhaga), of uncertain origin. See there for more.

ety: fr. ṭhagānā; — for āʼī see ṭhakurāʼī
def: s.f. Cheating, fraud, deceit, imposture, knavery; swindling, robbery, theft.
s.f. The business or practice of a ṭhag; robbery, cheating, imposture; — ṭhagī, the department for the suppression of ṭhags.
s.m. A combination of five short syllables.
v.n. To be cheated, &c.; — v.t. To cheat, deceive, &c. ( = ṭhagnā); — to cause to be cheated, &c. ( = ṭhagwānā)
s.f. Cheating, cheat, trick, imposture (see next).
s.f. The business or practice of a ṭhag; robbery, cheating, imposture; — ṭhagī, the department for the suppression of ṭhags.
s.f. = ṭhagnī, q.v. s.f. The wife of a ṭhag; a female robber or cheat, &c.
part. adj. Stopped; remaining still; motionless.
s.m. A cheat, impostor, &c. = ṭhag, q.v. s.m. A robber, assassin, garotter, cut-throat; one of a gang who strangle or poison travellers; deceiver, impostor, cheat, knave, sharper, swindler, plunderer
subs: s.f. Cunning, trickery, roguery, fraudulent dealing, &c. (see ṭhagāʼī)
s.f. The art of tricking or cheating; fraudulent arts, swindling, &c. (see ṭhagāʼī).

ety: ṭhag˚ = S. स्थग(ति)+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cheat, deceive, beguile, impose on, delude, circumvent, trick, dupe; to rob; to be stolen, to be made captive, to be captivated
s.f. The wife of a ṭhag; a female robber or cheat, &c.
v.t. To cause to be cheated or robbed, &c.
s.f. Bottom; depth; end; ford
subs: (-kī), To take the depth (of), to sound.

def: s.m. Loud laughter, a roar or peal of laughter; loud noise, a succession of loud sounds, peal, burst, crash, explosion
subs: v.n. To laugh loud, to burst out laughing.

def: s.m. = ṭhahar; s.f. = ṭahal; qq.v.
s.f. = ṭhahar, q.v.
s.m. (prov.) See ṭhahrāʼo.
v.n. = thar-tharānā, q.v. v.n. To shake, tremble, shiver, quiver, shudder, quake; to vibrate; to totter.

v.n. To stand; to stand still; to stand firm; to be stationary; to be fixed; to be stopped; to be congealed, be frozen; to stop, rest, pause, cease, desist; to stay, remain, abide, wait, tarry; to last, endure; to be ascertained, be proved, be established; to be settled, be agreed upon, be concluded; to be fixed on, be determined, be resolved; to prove to be, to turn out
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀞𑀳𑀺𑀭𑀇 (*ṭhahiraï), denominal verb from Sanskrit *स्त॒भि॒र (*stabhirá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *stabʰrás, from Proto-Indo-European *stebʰ-ró-s, from *stebʰ- + *-rós.

def: s.f. Fordableness; — adj. Fordable.

v.t. To cause to stand or stay, to stop; to ascertain; to establish, prove, demonstrate; to conclude, consider; to ascribe; to appropriate; to fix, settle, decide, determine; to decree, judge, adjudge; to fix on, appoint, constitute
subs: ṭhahrā-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = ṭhahrānā.

ety: fr. ṭhahrānā; — āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Fixture; settlement, agreement; appropriation; proof, conclusion; determination, decision; stability, permanence, durability; cessation, pause, rest, stop, halt.

ety: ṭhahrā, rt. of ṭhahrā-nā+ū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Stable, permanent, durable, lasting ( = ṭikāʼū).
s.f. A mass, heap, pile (of cakes, clothes, &c.).
adj. Firm, solid, compact; stable, permanent, durable; ascertained, established, well-founded, certain, positive, to be relied on; fixed, settled, determined on; right, just, correct, exact.

ety: Prk. ठाणं, or ठाणु, or ठाउ, or ठामु; S. स्थानं, or स्थामं
def: adv. From place to place; in different places; here and there.
s.m. A large bag, a sack.
s.f. A bag, a sack; a purse of a thousand rupees ( = toṛā); the scrotum.
s.f. A bag, a sack; a purse of a thousand rupees ( = toṛā); the scrotum.

ety: Prk. ठाणं, or ठाणु, or ठाउ, or ठामु; S. स्थानं, or स्थामं
def: adv. From place to place; in different places; here and there.
s.m. An instrument with a flat blade and long handle used to press down cakes, &c., on the pan.
s.m. A boundary pillar built of masonry (in opp. to ḍhūʼā, 'a mud pillar').

ety: Prk. थक्को and थक्कओ; see thaknā

subs: adj. Conglomerated, stuck together; thickly clustered, jumbled; wet through, soaked, drenched
adj. = thakkā; — adv. In the mass, or heap, in the lump or cluster.

subs: thakā-pikkā, thakā-phakkā, adj. Worn out, troubled, perplexed, distracted, confounded.

def: s.f. Weariness, fatigue, exhaustion, lassitude.
s.f. Weariness, fatigue, exhaustion, lassitude.

ety: thakā, q.v.+ān = Prk. अअणं = S. त्वं
def: s.f. = thakan; thakāʼī, q.v.

def: s.m. See ṭhākur

ety: thak˚ = Prk. थक्के(इ), fr. S. स्तभ् (acc. स्तप्)+कृ;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be wearied, to weary, tire, be fatigued, be jaded, be exhausted; to flag, droop, faint; to be enervated; to be inert, sluggish; to be slack or dull

Prakrit 𑀣𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (thakkaï) Apabhramsa थक्कइ (thakkaï) Urdu تھکنا Inherited from Apabhramsa थक्कइ (thakkaï), from Prakrit 𑀣𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (thakkaï), denominal verb from 𑀣𑀓𑁆𑀓 (thakka), from Sanskrit स्थ (stha), from स्था (sthā), f…

ety: ṭhak˚ = Prk. टक्ख(इ) = S. तक्ष(ति), rt. तक्ष्
def: v.t. To knock, &c. ( = ṭhakṭhakānā, q.v.). v.t. To knock, rap, tap, pat, sound (cf. khaṭkhaṭānā).
adj. See s.v. thak. s.m. A boundary pillar built of masonry (in opp. to ḍhūʼā, 'a mud pillar').
s.m. A sound of knocking or hammering, knock, rap, tap; — hard work, troublesome business, harassing labour, difficulty, awkward or untoward affair (syn. bakheṛā).
s.m. A sound of knocking or hammering, knock, rap, tap; — hard work, troublesome business, harassing labour, difficulty, awkward or untoward affair (syn. bakheṛā).

ety: by redupl. of Prk. टक्ख(इ) = S. तक्ष(ति), rt. तक्ष्
def: v.t. To knock, rap, tap, pat, sound (cf. khaṭkhaṭānā).
s.m. A stickler, wrangler.
s.f. A kind of small boat, a shallop, skiff (syn. pansoʼī).

ety: thak˚, rt. of ṭhaknā, redupl.+elā = Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Stuffed together in a crowd, crowded ( = maćāmać).

s.m. An idol, a deity; the Supreme God; a lord, master, chief (among certain castes of Rājpūts); a feudal noble of Rājputānā; the head of a tribe or village; a landholder; a person of rank or authority, one entitled to reverence or respect; an honorific title after the name of a distinguished person (in Bengal, written Tāgore); a title of respect, Sir, Master, Your Worship; an honorific form of address for a barber (cf. ḵẖalīfa, 'a tailor')
subs: s.m. A name for the Deity (among Bairāgīs); a title of respect
s.f. lit. 'The service of an idol'; a grant of revenue for the support of an idol-temple

def: s.f. The rank or office of ṭhākur; lordship; the office of a barber (cf. ṭhakurāʼī, ṭhukrāʼī).
s.f. Godship, divinity; the rank or status of ṭhākur; lordliness, nobility; — the office of barber (see ṭhākurāʼī).
s.f. = ṭhākurāʼin; ṭhākurānī.
s.f. A goddess; a ṭhākur's wife or daughter; a lady; a title of respect, Madam; a barber's wife.
s.f. = ṭhākurāʼin, q.v. s.f. A goddess; a ṭhākur's wife or daughter; a lady; a title of respect, Madam; a barber's wife.
s.f. The wife of a ṭhākur, &c. = ṭhākurānī, q.v. s.f. = ṭhākurāʼin, q.v. s.f. A goddess; a ṭhākur's wife or daughter; a lady; a title of respect, Madam; a barber's wife.
s.m. Firm or dry ground, land (as opp. to water); ford; ground or land, soil; place, spot, site; den of a lion or tiger; raised ground, a mound; a sandhill
subs: s.m. Ford or boat; — place of abode; the whereabouts (of a person); (fig.) means of attaining one's desires
thal-ćārī, adj. & s.m. Moving on land, land, terrestrial (animal); — a land animal.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀣𑀮 (thala, “dry place, place”), from Sanskrit स्थल (sthála, “dry land, place”). Cognates include Punjabi تَھࣇ (thaḷ), Nepali थल (thal), Assamese থল (thol), Odia ଥଳ (thaḷa), Old Awadhi थल (thala), Gujarati થળ (thaḷ), Marwari थळ (thaḷ), Marathi थळ (thaḷ).

def: s.m. A large flat plate or dish of metal (usually brass, or bell-metal); a tray; — (thālā), the basin or trench dug round the root of a tree for holding water; the excavation in which a tree is to be planted.
s.f. The state of being out of employment; freedom from occupation, disengagedness; leisure; exemption from labour, holiday.
s.m. A leafy branch (esp. one lopped off; cf. ḍāl).
s.m. = thaṛā, q.v. s.m. Sithing place, seat, platform (cf. thar).

s.m. A large flat plate or dish of metal (usually brass, or bell-metal); a tray; — (thālā), the basin or trench dug round the root of a tree for holding water; the excavation in which a tree is to be planted.
s.m. A leafy branch (esp. one lopped off; cf. ḍāl).

r: ṭhālā, ṭhālī, ṭhāṛā, lit., ṭhālā phirnā
def: v.n. To wander about idly, to saunter about
subs: v.n. To be unemployed, be idle; to be disengaged, to be at leisure.

ety: thalak˚ = S. श्र्लथ+कृ; or a var. pron. of kharak or pharak, q.v.
def: v.n. To shake, &c. = thal-thalānā; — to tremble, quiver, flutter, throb, palpitate ( = dhaṛaknā).

s.f. A small flat plate or dish of metal or earth; plate, dish, platter; a salver; a stand (for a candlestick, or a ḥuqqa, &c.); (fig.) a present of sweetmeats, fruits, &c. (made by relations or friends on festive occasions)
subs: v.n. To strike or play on a thālī or plate.

def: s.f. A basin or trench dug round the root of a tree (for holding water); a mound about the root of a tree.
s.f. A small branch; a sprig, spray.

r: ṭhālī, ṭhālā, ṭhāṛā, lit., ṭhālā phirnā
def: v.n. To wander about idly, to saunter about
subs: v.n. To be unemployed, be idle; to be disengaged, to be at leisure.

ety: S. स्थाल+इका, or dim. of thālā
def: s.f. = thālī, q.v. s.f. A small flat plate or dish of metal or earth; plate, dish, platter; a salver; a stand (for a candlestick, or a ḥuqqa, &c.); (fig.) a present of sweetmeats, fruits, &c. (made by relations or friends on festive occasions)
subs: v.n. To strike or play on a thālī or plate.

def: adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid, soft, squashy; hanging loosely, baggy; shaky, waving, undulating, fluctuating (as a jelly, &c.); — s.f. Shaking loosely, being flabby, flabbiness, &c.
subs: v.n. To shake (as flabby flesh), &c. = thalthalānā, q.v.

def: adj. = thal-thal, q.v. adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid, soft, squashy; hanging loosely, baggy; shaky, waving, undulating, fluctuating (as a jelly, &c.); — s.f. Shaking loosely, being flabby, flabbiness, &c.
subs: v.n. To shake (as flabby flesh), &c. = thalthalānā, q.v.

def: v.n. To shake, to hang loosely (as flabby flesh or fat), to shake, undulate, or fluctuate (as a jelly, or any thick glutinous substance).
adj. Out of employment; idle ( = ṭhālā); — s.m. An idle fellow.
